item.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side.
The procedures listed below are for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Support the front No. 1 suspension lower arm with a jack using a wooden block to avoid damage.
Remove the nut and separate the front stabilizer link assembly from the front shock absorber.

atten4.ptxt: If the ball joint turns together with the nut, use a hexagon wrench (6 mm) to hold the stud.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT SHOCK ABSORBER WITH COIL SPRING

s2.ptxt: Loosen the lock nut of the front shock absorber with coil spring.

item.ptxt: Do not remove the lock nut.
Only loosen the nut when disassembling the front shock absorber with coil spring.

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the front flexible hose and front speed sensor wire harness from the front shock absorber with coil spring.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to remove the front speed sensor from the front shock absorber with coil spring.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 nuts on the lower side of the front shock absorber with coil spring.

item.ptxt: When removing the nuts, keep the bolts from rotating.
Keep the bolts inserted to secure the front axle assembly.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 3 nuts on the upper side of the front shock absorber with coil spring.
Lower the front axle assembly, and remove the 2 bolts on the lower side of the front shock absorber.
Remove the front shock absorber with coil spring.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the front speed sensor is disconnected from the front shock absorber with coil spring.
